What Stays ? @Frag_Growth . 

The library was on the ground floor of the building where he grew up. His father went there for detective novels.

@jenyavyguzov went for something else.

Art books. Fashion magazines. Images from worlds he couldn't yet explain, only feel.

He was born in Krasnoyarsk, Siberia, into a working-class family. Far from the cities those magazines came from, but close enough to imagine them.

He began collecting discarded pages, cutting people, landscapes and textures into collages that gave shape to feelings he didn't yet have words for.

His family imagined a more practical future. He tried financial college, left after two years, worked as a waiter, sold advertising, and kept making art whenever life allowed him to.

Eventually, fashion took him abroad as a model.

The job paid the bills. The cities changed his life.

~ The Flower That Stayed

Travel gave Jenya something more valuable than opportunity.

It taught him to look.

Every city carried its own language cracked walls, peeling paint, worn concrete, rusted metal, forgotten surfaces shaped by years of weather and people passing through.

Most saw decay, He saw texture.

He photographed everything. Without realizing it, he was building an archive.

In 2015, a flower appeared in his work.

It has never left, Not painted, Not illustrated. BUILT. 

Every petal is assembled by hand from photographs of real textures collected across Europe and Asia. Walls become petals. Rust becomes color. Concrete becomes softness. Places that had never met find themselves blooming together.

A visual diary disguised as a flower.

Years later came Vogue, GQ Style, Tatler, Elle, Adidas, Puma, Lacoste, IKEA, exhibitions across Europe, educational lectures, and an invitation to create the official artwork for the Paris Prints Textile Show.

The recognition mattered.

But the process never changed.

He still builds the same way he always has.

One fragment at a time.

~ Fragments of Growth Is Not a just a Collection. 

It is a language built over more than a decade. Every flower is assembled by hand from photographs of real textures Jenya captured across cities and forgotten surfaces around the world. No generative AI. No algorithms. Just fragments, collected over time and composed into something entirely new.

The blockchain is simply where that journey continues. 

Through limited drops, evolving collector mechanics, and works from Jenya's personal archive, Fragments of Growth invites others into the story. Because every petal remembers a place, every texture remembers time, and every artwork proves the same quiet idea, beauty isn't created it is discovered.

@Frag_Growth is that film, one petal at a time. 🌸

Top AI Training Platforms Open to Nigerians (2026)

1. Outlier AI — Best paying overall
Pays $15–$40/hour depending on your expertise. Great for STEM, law, medicine, finance, and creative writing specialists. Apply at outlier. ai.

2. Mindrift AI — Best for beginners
Connects professionals with AI companies to write prompts, correct responses, and improve accuracy. Pays around $300/week via Payoneer (available to Nigerians).

3. Data Annotation Tech — Best for high earners
Pays $35–$65/hour based on skill level. No tech experience needed, but you must be detail-oriented. Requires a laptop and stable internet.

4. Pareto AI — Best for career progression
Start with simple review tasks and move up to advanced projects. Pays weekly via PayPal (available in Nigeria), with bonuses for accurate contributors.

5. Remotasks — Best for beginners building up
Focuses on image labeling, LiDAR annotation, and AI response evaluation. Beginners earn $3–$8/hour, rising to $10–$15/hour with experience. Pays via Payoneer weekly.

6. Clickworker — Best for variety
Tasks include text creation, audio transcription, image tagging, and web research. Basic tasks pay $3–$7/hour; complex tasks pay $8–$15/hour. Also gives access to Microsoft's UHRS platform.

7. OpenTrain AI — Best for freelancers
A marketplace where you set your own rates and work across multiple clients. Payments go through a secure escrow system with fast payouts, usually within a day.

8. OneForma (Pactera EDGE) — Best for language tasks
Great for data annotation, search relevance evaluation, and linguistic QA. Basic tasks pay $5–$10/hour; specialized language projects pay significantly more.

Hiring ‼️
🎥AI Video Creator
📍Remote
💵 Salary: N250,000 naira/month

You’ll be responsible for turning outfit photos into styled videos on one consistent AI model - she must look realistic, not artificial or “AI-generated.” You’ll maintain her same face, body, and look across all videos while styling her in different outfits based on briefs and creative direction, without needing a human model or physical shoot.

Requirements‼️

•Proficient in AI video generation tools (e.g., Runway, Kling, Luma, Pika, Sora, or similar)
•Skilled in AI image generation/editing to maintain a consistent, realistic model across videos
•Strong eye for fashion styling, trends, and aesthetics
•Able to follow content briefs and recreate TikTok-style formats and pacing
•Strong attention to detail, clean transitions, consistent lighting/style, no AI artifacts or glitches
•Reliable and able to deliver on an agreed content schedule

Expectations:

•Maintain one consistent AI model (same face, body, look) across all content
•Ensure the model looks fully realistic, no obvious or “AI-generated” appearance
•Ensure movement, gestures, and expressions look smooth and human-like, not stiff or glitchy

How to Apply:
Send your portfolio/sample AI-generated videos (fashion content) and a list of tools you work with to aivisualmastery@gmail.com

AI app ideas are cheap. Launchable templates are the bottleneck.

Awesome Generative AI Apps is a curated GitHub repo of 50+ open-source generative AI app templates for builders who want to clone, deploy, and adapt AI SaaS products.

It helps you skip the blank Next.js project by grouping templates for image, video, beauty/fashion, e-commerce, writing, agents, audio, and integrations, with setup and deploy paths already documented.

Key features:

• 50+ app templates – cloneable products across image generation, video generation, virtual try-on, product photography, content, agents, and more
• SaaS plumbing included – README highlights Stripe billing, Google OAuth, Prisma/PostgreSQL, credit balances, and job history
• Vercel deployment path – templates point to Deploy to Vercel buttons and a quick-start flow
• Category navigation – table of contents lets you scan by use case instead of hunting through random demos
• MIT license – repo metadata and README both mark it as MIT licensed for reuse and adaptation

It’s open-source (MIT license).

Link in the reply 👇

Diffusion language models, which generate text by repeatedly filling in masked parts rather than writing strictly from left to right, are becoming a serious alternative to standard transformer language models, but the usual ways of improving reasoning after training were built for left-to-right generation and do not fit the diffusion models.

The authors of this paper introduce a self-training method in which the model first produces its own answer, then uses parts of that completed answer as helpful context while learning how earlier denoising steps should behave.

Instead of comparing teacher and student one next token at a time, it compares them at each denoising step, which matches how diffusion language models actually generate text. 

Across math and planning tasks, d-OPSD matches or beats supervised finetuning and reinforcement-learning baselines, while needing far fewer optimization steps than the main RL baseline.

https://t.co/eRqYj4YPwi

15-YEAR-OLD BOUGHT A $5,000 MAC STUDIO AND IN 30 DAYS BUILT A FREE AI TOOLKIT THAT REPLACES $236/MONTH IN SUBSCRIPTIONS

30 days, one Mac Studio M3, every top LLM tested on every part of his business - and he decided not to hide it behind a paywall

connected N8n and ManyChat to handle and translate all DMs locally and automatically - zero cloud APIs, zero data leaving the machine

$3,000 invested in llmacademy com and made it free - 30+ tools, GitHub files, Ollama, OpenAI Codex, model leaderboards - everything in one place

text to video, text to image, research agent, uncensored LLM leaderboard - tools that separately cost hundreds per month now completely free

$5,000 Mac Studio, $3,000 on the academy, zero paywall - and a 15-year-old who decided this should be available to everyone

🎯 Limitations:

RL finetuning is necessary for stability but sacrifices motion diversity enabled by diffusion motion generator. Motion generation on-the-fly is challenging at OOD cases and can still lead to infeasible (physically or untrackable) references.
